title: How to use the app
heroTitle: Pass the Finnish taxi exam — even with limited Finnish
heroSub: Five tools that work together: learn the words, spot the clues, practice real questions, and rehearse under exam conditions.
step1: Start with the Exam Guide so you know the format and the 4 categories.
step2: Build words in Vocabulary, then learn the Clue Words logic.
step3: Drill Topic Practice section by section — read every explanation.
step4: Rehearse with timed Model Tests and review every miss.
step5: Use Progress & Weak Areas to re-drill what you keep getting wrong.

examGuide.title: Exam Guide
examGuide.what: The facts of the real Traficom exam — format, the 4 official categories, and exam-day rules.
examGuide.how: Read this first so you know exactly what you are training for: 50 questions, 45 minutes, 38/50 to pass (with a minimum in each category), in Finnish or Swedish.

vocabulary.title: Vocabulary
vocabulary.what: Core Finnish taxi words grouped into bite-size sets, each with a flashcard lesson and a quiz.
vocabulary.how: Learn a set, then take its quiz. Build the base words before tackling full questions.

clueWords.title: Clue Words
clueWords.what: The answer-logic words that quietly point to the right or wrong option.
clueWords.how: Positive clues (varmistaa, huolehtia) lean correct; absolutes (aina, vain, koskaan) are usually traps. Train your eye here so you can answer even when you don’t understand every word.

topicPractice.title: Topic Practice
topicPractice.what: Real exam-style questions sorted into lessons under the 4 official categories.
topicPractice.how: Work through a section lesson by lesson. Every answer shows the translation and explanation, so each question teaches you something.

modelTests.title: Model Tests
modelTests.what: Full, timed mock exams that rehearse the real exam’s format and time pressure.
modelTests.how: Use these near exam day to rehearse under pressure. Sit one in a quiet 45 minutes, then review every question you missed.

progress.title: Progress & Weak Areas
progress.what: Tracks what you’ve practiced and surfaces the questions you keep getting wrong.
progress.how: Check it regularly and re-drill your weak areas until they stop showing up.

footer: Tip: the exam is in Finnish (or Swedish), with no translation into other languages. The clue-word method is your edge — most mistakes come from misreading, not from not knowing.
